title: Raging Bender

jokes.text: Good news, everyone! I've taught the toaster to feel love.
jokes.setup: Professor's signature 'Good news, everyone!' opening suggests something important
jokes.punchline: It's just teaching a kitchen appliance emotions
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor absurdity - his excitement over trivial mad science perfectly fits his character. The juxtaposition of 'good news' with something ridiculous is a running series gag.

jokes.text: Hermes liked it so much he decided to stay.
jokes.setup: Brain Slug planet sounds ominous
jokes.punchline: Hermes claims he 'liked' being mind-controlled
jokes.explanation: Standard mind control joke - the irony is obvious and the execution is competent but predictable.

jokes.text: Hermes has all the fun. He's got a Brain Slug on his head!
jokes.setup: Brain slugs are clearly dangerous parasites
jokes.punchline: Bender sees parasitic mind control as enviable fun
jokes.explanation: Bender's selfishness and obliviousness make this work - he genuinely thinks Hermes is lucky, which fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Switch to a garlic shampoo.
jokes.setup: Brain slugs are alien parasites
jokes.punchline: Treating them like earthly pests with folk remedy
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdity - treating advanced alien parasites like head lice is a nice unexpected twist. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: Walk around not wearing a helmet.
jokes.setup: Asking what they'll do on the dangerous Brain Slug planet
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests the most dangerous possible activity
jokes.explanation: The mind-controlled Hermes matter-of-factly suggesting everyone get infected is darkly funny and fits the possession trope well.

jokes.text: Sounds great, Hermes! Whatever you say.
jokes.setup: Hermes just suggested a plan that would get them all mind-controlled
jokes.punchline: The crew enthusiastically agrees without thinking
jokes.explanation: Standard group stupidity joke. Functional but predictable setup for them escaping.

jokes.text: Let's ditch him and go to the movies.
jokes.setup: Everyone enthusiastically agreeing with possessed Hermes
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning to abandon him
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - the crew's immediate betrayal of their brainwashed friend is callously funny.

jokes.text: I hate subtitles. Alien films are so pretentious.
jokes.setup: Looking at movie options in the future
jokes.punchline: Bender applies current film criticism clichés to future context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion - in a world full of aliens, 'alien films' become the pretentious foreign cinema. Perfectly captures Bender's lowbrow tastes.

jokes.text: Planet of the Clams. About an upside-down world where lobster is slave to clam.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg suggesting a movie everyone can enjoy
jokes.punchline: It's Planet of the Apes but with his own species dynamics
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Zoidberg naturally suggests a film about crustacean social hierarchy. The 'upside-down' description adds perfect absurdity.

jokes.text: Who invited you?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to be helpful with movie suggestions
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual cruelty to the group's outcast
jokes.explanation: Standard 'dump on Zoidberg' joke. Works because of character dynamics but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: For 25 cents less, get a super small.
jokes.setup: Customer ordering a small drink
jokes.punchline: Theater offers an even smaller size for tiny savings
jokes.explanation: Good satire of movie theater upselling tactics, but generic enough that any show could do it.

jokes.text: Give me a large diet malt liquor and a popcorn with extra motor oil.
jokes.setup: Robot ordering movie snacks
jokes.punchline: Orders alcohol and motor oil instead of human food
jokes.explanation: Solid robot-specific humor. Motor oil as a popcorn topping is a nice touch that shows Bender's mechanical nature.

jokes.text: Glagnar's Human Rinds presents: This Week in the Universe.
jokes.setup: Newsreel sponsor announcement
jokes.punchline: Snack food made from humans, casually mentioned
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. The pork rinds reference makes cannibalism seem mundane and commercial.

jokes.text: C. Randall Poopenmeyer
jokes.setup: Serious news about the mayor
jokes.punchline: His name is literally Poopenmeyer
jokes.explanation: Juvenile name humor. Gets a chuckle but it's the cheapest kind of comedy - just making a name sound like 'poop.'

jokes.text: Miss Universe Gladys Lennox entertains troops fighting to wipe out the human race. Go get 'em, boys!
jokes.setup: Beauty queen entertaining troops
jokes.punchline: The troops are fighting to commit genocide against humans
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - treating human extinction like a cheerful USO show. The casual 'Go get 'em, boys!' makes it perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: technical melting
jokes.setup: Robot fighting match result
jokes.punchline: Instead of technical knockout, it's technical melting
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay - adapts boxing terminology for robot combat. Clean and economical.

jokes.text: Mind taking your head off? - I need it to watch the movie.
jokes.setup: Polite request to remove head blocking view
jokes.punchline: Bender's literal response about needing his head
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure. The literal response works because robots might actually be able to remove their heads.

jokes.text: Ask Flabby here to describe it to you later. She is as the factory made her. They should have stopped about halfway through.
jokes.setup: Suggesting the blind person get help from Amy
jokes.punchline: Bender cruelly insults Amy's appearance with escalating meanness
jokes.explanation: Peak Bender cruelty - the escalation from 'Flabby' to the factory comment is perfectly in character, though the humor is mean-spirited.

jokes.text: If you want Calculon to race to the battle in his Hover Ferrari, press 1. If you want Calculon to double-check his paperwork, press 2.
jokes.setup: Action scene setup with explosion and chase
jokes.punchline: Interactive choice between exciting action or boring bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of both interactive TV and dramatic pacing. The mundane option in an action context is absurdly funny.

jokes.text: You have pressed 2. - I didn't! - I'm almost positive you did.
jokes.setup: Interactive TV system malfunction
jokes.punchline: TV insists Bender chose the boring option despite his denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-through on the interactive TV joke. The TV's certainty despite Bender's protest mirrors real tech frustrations.

jokes.text: Pardon me, sir. But you seem to be kicking my seat. - That's the gist of what I said. Would you mind?
jokes.setup: Polite complaint about seat kicking
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive response and continued rudeness
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Bender's dismissive attitude. The 'gist' line is particularly rude and dismissive.

jokes.text: Sir, I challenge you to fisticuffs.
jokes.setup: Movie theater conflict escalating
jokes.punchline: Formal old-fashioned duel challenge over seat kicking
jokes.explanation: The formal language contrasts nicely with the petty dispute. 'Fisticuffs' is an amusing word choice that elevates the trivial conflict.

jokes.text: Cripes! The Masked Unit! You knocked him out cold!
jokes.setup: Bender gets in random fight at movies
jokes.punchline: He accidentally defeated a famous robot fighter
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that drives the plot forward. The reveal that his opponent was famous adds stakes and comedy to the random encounter.

jokes.text: You should see me at funerals.
jokes.setup: Commissioner praising Bender for being the biggest jerk
jokes.punchline: Bender boasts about being inappropriate at funerals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bender line - perfectly captures his complete lack of social awareness and inappropriate behavior. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Float like a float-bot, sting like a stinging machine!
jokes.setup: Bender getting excited about robot fighting
jokes.punchline: Robot version of Muhammad Ali's famous phrase
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective adaptation of famous quote. Works because it fits the robot context, though it's fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Two robots enter, one robot leaves. The other leaves after being declared the winner.
jokes.setup: Setting up brutal fighting rules like Thunderdome
jokes.punchline: Anti-climactic clarification that both robots survive
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke - sets up Thunderdome expectations then deflates them perfectly. The structure is flawless with great timing.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure I did. Yes, definitely.
jokes.setup: Amy forgetting if she mentioned crippling pain
jokes.punchline: She becomes increasingly confident she definitely mentioned it
jokes.explanation: Mild escalation humor as Amy convinces herself. Works but isn't particularly memorable or character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, again with the orphanarium.
jokes.setup: Leela about to tell a personal story
jokes.punchline: Bender dismissively anticipates another sad orphan story
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Leela's tendency to share orphan trauma. Bender's dismissive attitude is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: This is more important than that marble-eating contest.
jokes.setup: Leela wants Bender to fight despite danger
jokes.punchline: Random reference to Bender previously eating marbles as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail about Bender's past. Marble-eating contest is perfectly random.

jokes.text: But I can beat these dorks with one eye closed.
jokes.setup: Leela boasting about her fighting ability
jokes.punchline: She literally only has one eye
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor - the common expression becomes literally impossible for Leela. Perfect integration of her physical trait with the joke.

jokes.text: No girl has the will of a warrior. You have the will of a housewife. At best, the schoolmarm.
jokes.setup: Sexist martial arts master dismissing Leela
jokes.punchline: Escalating from housewife to the even more insulting 'schoolmarm'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the villain effectively. The escalation to 'schoolmarm' adds period flavor, though the sexism is fairly standard.

jokes.text: The winner? Me! Rematch? You lose again! Had enough? I thought so!
jokes.setup: Funog claiming victory through mental warrior will
jokes.punchline: He wins multiple imaginary fights in rapid succession
jokes.explanation: Great escalation as Funog gets carried away with his philosophy. The rapid-fire victories against an imaginary opponent is absurd and funny.

jokes.text: She's right. I don't want to end up like her!
jokes.setup: Leela inspiring Bender with her story of lost opportunity
jokes.punchline: Bender completely misses the point and insults her instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - he gets motivated but for completely the wrong reasons. His obliviousness to Leela's feelings is both funny and cruel.

jokes.text: Still can't reach!
jokes.setup: Bender attempting to touch his toes during training
jokes.punchline: His inflexible robot body can't bend that way
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy based on robot limitations. Works because it's specific to Bender's mechanical nature.

jokes.text: From Mom's Friendly Robot Factory in America's heartland, Mexico
jokes.setup: Traditional fight announcement format
jokes.punchline: Mexico is now considered America's heartland
jokes.explanation: Clever geographical absurdity that suggests future border changes. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I got you a Bender hat. - Wow! Thanks, Hermes! I... Hey, cut that out!
jokes.setup: Hermes giving Bender a supportive hat
jokes.punchline: The brain slug is presumably doing something to manipulate the situation
jokes.explanation: Serviceable callback to the brain slug subplot

jokes.text: You can't hit what you can't see.
jokes.setup: Invisible/transparent robot opponent
jokes.punchline: States the obvious advantage of being invisible
jokes.explanation: Straightforward invisible fighter joke. The obviousness of the statement is part of the humor, but it's fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Quit scratching your ax-hole and get back.
jokes.setup: Bender has an ax stuck in his back
jokes.punchline: Leela creates crude pun combining 'axe-hole' and 'asshole'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - literal ax hole and the implied insult. Crude but inventive.

jokes.text: He's not dead! What's up?
jokes.setup: Bender thinking he killed his opponent
jokes.punchline: Casual greeting after supposedly being beaten to death
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. The casual 'what's up?' after a death scare is nicely understated.

jokes.text: Ultimate Robot Fighting is a scam. Keep it under your head. But the most popular robot always wins.
jokes.setup: Revelation about the fighting being fake
jokes.punchline: 'Keep it under your head' instead of 'keep it under your hat'
jokes.explanation: Robot-specific twist on common expression. The wrestling/boxing comparison adds to the sports entertainment satire.

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo, I'm popular!
jokes.setup: Learning he won due to popularity, not skill
jokes.punchline: Bender focuses on being popular instead of being deceived
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - completely misses the point that he's being manipulated, just happy to be liked.

jokes.text: You're pure lowest common denominator.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Bender is popular
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of his appeal to the masses
jokes.explanation: Brutally accurate description of Bender's character. Works as both insult and character analysis.

jokes.text: I'm just an ex-con trying to go straight and get my kids back.
jokes.setup: Bender adopting wrestling persona
jokes.punchline: Claims to have children and be reformed
jokes.explanation: Excellent wrestling trope parody. The idea of Bender having children he's trying to get back is absurd and perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: I'm not from here! I have my own customs! Look at my crazy passport!
jokes.setup: Generic foreign heel wrestler character
jokes.punchline: Reduces all foreign culture to 'crazy passport'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of xenophobic wrestling tropes. The passport bit perfectly captures how lazy these characters usually are.

jokes.text: I love smoking. And after I win the fight, I'm headed straight to your favorite restaurant.
jokes.setup: Wrestler who loves unhealthy habit
jokes.punchline: Threatens to smoke at the audience's favorite places
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation - makes smoking personal to the audience. Good heel heat generation through relatable annoyance.

jokes.text: Well, I need floozies. Let's roll.
jokes.setup: Bender dismissing his friends for fame
jokes.punchline: Casually admits he just wants groupies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender honesty - he doesn't pretend his motivations are noble. The casual 'let's roll' makes it even more dismissive.

jokes.text: Those morons! I said peaberry, not sandalwood!
jokes.setup: Bender's bath soap sales are down
jokes.punchline: He's passionate about soap scent varieties like a diva
jokes.explanation: Great celebrity diva parody - Bender treating soap scents like they're crucial artistic choices. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: The Gender Bender! The most unpopular fighter since Sergeant Feces Processor.
jokes.setup: Introducing Bender's new unpopular persona
jokes.punchline: Previous worst fighter had an even more disgusting name
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor with the feces reference, but the escalation works. The name is memorably gross.

jokes.text: I thought it was real, like pro wrestling. But it's fixed, like boxing.
jokes.setup: Fry's confusion about which sports are real
jokes.punchline: Gets it backwards - thinks wrestling is real and boxing is fixed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that perfectly captures Fry's naive worldview. The structure is flawless and it works as both character comedy and sports commentary.

jokes.text: I'm a real toughie.
jokes.setup: Bender in tutu trying to be intimidating
jokes.punchline: Delivers tough guy line while dressed femininely
jokes.explanation: Basic visual comedy from the contrast between appearance and statement. Works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Not Funog! Let's hit the gym. I'll teach you to fight like a girl!
jokes.setup: Leela agreeing to train Bender after rejecting him
jokes.punchline: Reclaiming 'fight like a girl' as positive after the master's sexism
jokes.explanation: Great character arc payoff. Leela reclaiming the insult as empowerment works well.

jokes.text: My memory is not so good, but I think the Earth was destroyed.
jokes.setup: Discussing Ali vs 80-foot Joe Frazier fight
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions possible Earth destruction as memory issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - treating planetary destruction as a minor memory lapse. The casual delivery makes it hilarious.

jokes.text: A bet of $0 on Bender pays $1000 if he wins. Still, very few takers.
jokes.setup: Extremely long odds on Bender winning
jokes.punchline: Even free money has no takers, then explains why
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - even when betting is free, people won't take it. The 'It's not a smart bet' explanation makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Got it. Large kickle, hold the tickle.
jokes.setup: Leela's instructions to kick instead of tickle
jokes.punchline: Bender turns it into fast-food ordering language
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Bender's food-service background bleeding through. The rhyme and rhythm make it very quotable.

jokes.text: Just let me put on my reading glasses. Why, Zoidberg! There's a lovely photo of you in here!
jokes.setup: Professor putting on reading glasses to read program
jokes.punchline: The 'photo' is actually Zoidberg himself that he's looking at
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection visual gag. Professor's senility makes the confusion believable and funny.

jokes.text: the gizmo from Pismo Beach!
jokes.setup: Dramatic fighter introduction
jokes.punchline: Reduces intimidating opponent to casual 'gizmo' from beach town
jokes.explanation: Nice anticlimax in the announcement. 'Gizmo' deflates the drama, and Pismo Beach is an unexpectedly mundane origin.

jokes.text: Final boarding call for Flight 406, non-stop service to pain.
jokes.setup: Pre-fight intimidation
jokes.punchline: Airline announcement format for violence threat
jokes.explanation: Creative threat delivery using airline language. The formality contrasts well with the violent intent.

jokes.text: It's a good time to bring up my new grill. With its patented design, the fat drains directly into my mouth.
jokes.setup: Mid-fight commentary break
jokes.punchline: Foreman promotes grill that defeats its health purpose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific parody - George Foreman's famous grill promotion twisted into something grotesque. Perfect timing and character integration.

jokes.text: You're just controlling him like a puppet. I mean, cheating in a fake fight. That's low.
jokes.setup: Leela accusing Funog of cheating
jokes.punchline: Escalation that cheating in fake fighting is somehow worse
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the absurdity of having ethics in fake fighting

jokes.text: See you in girl hell! I'll be in boy hell, much nicer.
jokes.setup: Funog's final words before defeat
jokes.punchline: Assumes afterlife has gender segregation and boy version is better
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist theology - gender segregated afterlife with quality differences. Perfect final expression of Funog's sexism.

jokes.text: Interesting side note: As a head without a body, I envy the dead.
jokes.setup: Post-fight commentary
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions existential horror of his condition
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark throwaway line that adds horror to what should be a victory moment. The casual delivery makes it hilarious.

jokes.text: Poor little guy starved to death.
jokes.setup: Asked about missing brain slug
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions parasite death from lack of brains to feed on
jokes.explanation: Dark callback that implies Fry's stupidity literally starved the parasite. Perfect character-specific insult disguised as sympathy.

jokes.text: But I beat up someone who hurt my feelings in high school.
jokes.setup: Leela saying she's proud despite Bender losing
jokes.punchline: She got revenge on her high school bully through proxy violence
jokes.explanation: Great character payoff. Leela's real motivation was personal revenge, which is perfectly petty and human.

jokes.text: Here's a card good for 10% off at Bed Bath & Beyond.
jokes.setup: Reward for losing fighter
jokes.punchline: Mundane discount coupon instead of prize money
jokes.explanation: Great anticlimax - the most mundane possible reward for a brutal fight. The specificity of the store makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That he did surely not.
jokes.setup: Someone says Bender didn't look bad in the tutu
jokes.punchline: Hermes' awkward grammar from brain slug influence
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the brain slug subplot. The awkward grammar suggests lingering effects.

tmdb_overview: Bender is the featured attraction in the Ultimate Robot Fighting League, but his glory is short-lived when he is instructed to intentionally lose his next match. Leela vows to lead Bender to victory, in an effort to show up her unsupportive martial arts teacher.
